contract_threat_patterns.attack_scenario: Customer builds ML pipeline on provider proprietary framework. Custom model formats, proprietary feature store, and platform-specific inference APIs. Migration requires rebuilding entire ML stack. Estimated cost exceeds 3 years of contract value.
contract_threat_patterns.detection: Review exit provisions for AI-specific portability. Check for model export formats, data export, and training pipeline portability.
contract_threat_patterns.mitigation: Require standard model export formats (ONNX, PMML). Include training data and feature definitions in export. Provider assistance for migration. Transition period with continued access. Cap transition charges.
contract_threat_patterns.agent_use: Risk Assessor: flag when AI platform contract lacks model portability or standard export format requirements.

contract_threat_patterns.name: AI Explainability Compliance Gap
contract_threat_patterns.description: AI provider unable to provide explainability required by EU AI Act or sector regulations, creating compliance gap for deployer.
contract_threat_patterns.attack_scenario: Financial institution deploys AI credit scoring from provider. Regulator requires explanation of individual decisions. Provider model is black-box. Cannot provide required explanations. Institution fined for non-compliance.
contract_threat_patterns.detection: Review explainability commitments against regulatory requirements. Check for individual decision explanation capability. Verify documentation obligations.
contract_threat_patterns.mitigation: Require explainability at individual decision level. Include regulatory explanation format compliance. Provider obligation to support regulatory inquiries. Technical documentation of model logic.
contract_threat_patterns.agent_use: Contract Reviewer: flag when high-risk AI application lacks individual decision explainability commitment.

contract_threat_patterns.name: AI Synthetic Data Quality Liability
contract_threat_patterns.description: AI-generated synthetic data used for testing or training contains statistical artefacts that lead to flawed business decisions.
contract_threat_patterns.attack_scenario: Provider generates synthetic customer data for testing. Data has hidden correlation bias not present in real data. Customer trains production model on synthetic data. Model performs well on synthetic test data but fails on real data, causing significant misallocated resources.
contract_threat_patterns.detection: Review synthetic data quality warranties. Check for statistical validation requirements. Verify fitness-for-purpose commitments.
contract_threat_patterns.mitigation: Include statistical fidelity warranty for synthetic data. Require documented validation methodology. Customer right to independent validation. Liability for decisions made on defective synthetic data.
contract_threat_patterns.agent_use: Risk Assessor: flag when synthetic data service lacks quality warranties or validation methodology.

contract_threat_patterns.name: Foundation Model License Scope Creep
contract_threat_patterns.description: Foundation model license terms restrict commercial use cases not apparent at time of licensing, creating compliance risk.
contract_threat_patterns.attack_scenario: Company licenses open-weight foundation model under permissive-seeming license. Deploys for customer-facing product. License actually prohibits use in high-risk domains. Company unknowingly violates license in healthcare application. Faces injunction and damages.
contract_threat_patterns.detection: Review foundation model license for: use case restrictions, acceptable use policies, downstream distribution rights, and modification rights.
contract_threat_patterns.mitigation: Comprehensive license audit before deployment. Map all intended use cases against license restrictions. Obtain explicit waivers for borderline use cases. Include downstream license compliance in vendor contracts.
contract_threat_patterns.agent_use: Contract Reviewer: flag when foundation model license has not been mapped against intended use cases.

contract_threat_patterns.name: Greenwashing Liability Through Supply Chain Claims
contract_threat_patterns.description: Company makes environmental claims based on supplier representations that prove false, creating greenwashing liability.
contract_threat_patterns.attack_scenario: Company markets product as carbon-neutral based on supplier offsetting certificates. Certificates prove fraudulent. Company faces regulatory action under Green Claims Directive and consumer class action. Supplier provides no indemnity.
contract_threat_patterns.detection: Review ESG claim verification mechanisms. Check for supplier warranty on environmental data. Verify indemnity for false ESG claims.
contract_threat_patterns.mitigation: Require third-party verified ESG data from suppliers. Include specific indemnity for false environmental claims. Audit rights over supplier ESG data. Right to retract claims and terminate if data is false.
contract_threat_patterns.agent_use: Risk Assessor: flag when environmental claims rely on unverified supplier data without indemnity.

contract_threat_patterns.name: Scope 3 Emissions Data Manipulation
contract_threat_patterns.description: Supplier manipulates emissions data to appear more sustainable, distorting customer Scope 3 reporting obligations.
contract_threat_patterns.attack_scenario: Customer reports Scope 3 emissions using supplier-provided data. Supplier underreports by 60 percent using non-standard methodology. Customer regulatory filing contains material misstatement. CSRD enforcement action initiated.
contract_threat_patterns.detection: Review emissions data methodology requirements. Check for third-party verification. Verify consistency with recognised standards (GHG Protocol).
contract_threat_patterns.mitigation: Require GHG Protocol-compliant methodology. Third-party verified emissions data. Annual sustainability audit rights. Indemnity for regulatory action caused by false data. Contractual emissions reduction targets.
contract_threat_patterns.agent_use: Contract Reviewer: flag when Scope 3 data obligations lack methodology specification or third-party verification.

contract_threat_patterns.name: EUDR Deforestation Due Diligence Chain Break
contract_threat_patterns.description: Supply chain deforestation due diligence breaks at sub-supplier level, creating EUDR non-compliance risk.
contract_threat_patterns.attack_scenario: Importer contracts with processor who provides deforestation-free certification. Processor sub-sources from smallholders with no traceability. EUDR inspection reveals supply chain gap. Importer faces product seizure and fine up to 4 percent of EU turnover.
contract_threat_patterns.detection: Review EUDR compliance obligations in supply contracts. Check for full traceability to plot of land. Verify due diligence statement provisions.
contract_threat_patterns.mitigation: Require geolocation data for all sourced commodities. Full supply chain mapping to origin. Satellite monitoring integration. Right to audit entire supply chain. Immediate termination for non-compliance.
contract_threat_patterns.agent_use: STRIDE Enumerator: flag when EUDR-relevant commodity supply chain lacks traceability to origin.

contract_threat_patterns.name: Modern Slavery Statement Enforcement Gap
contract_threat_patterns.description: Supply chain modern slavery provisions exist on paper but lack enforcement mechanisms, creating human rights liability.
contract_threat_patterns.attack_scenario: Company supply chain audit reveals forced labour in tier-3 supplier. Company modern slavery statement claims zero tolerance. Lack of contractual audit rights below tier-1 prevents verification. NGO expose causes reputational crisis.
contract_threat_patterns.detection: Review modern slavery provisions for: audit depth, cascade requirements, remediation obligations, and worker access to grievance mechanisms.
contract_threat_patterns.mitigation: Cascade modern slavery requirements to all supply chain tiers. Unannounced audit rights. Worker grievance mechanisms. Remediation-first approach with termination as last resort. Annual due diligence reporting.
contract_threat_patterns.agent_use: Risk Assessor: flag when modern slavery provisions do not cascade beyond tier-1 suppliers.

contract_threat_patterns.name: ESG Termination Trigger Avoidance Through Technicality
contract_threat_patterns.description: Supplier avoids ESG-related termination by technically meeting minimum thresholds while violating spirit of commitments.
contract_threat_patterns.attack_scenario: Contract requires supplier to maintain B Corp certification. Supplier loses certification but immediately applies for recertification. Claims certification is pending, not lost. Avoids termination trigger while operating without certification for 18 months.
contract_threat_patterns.detection: Review ESG termination triggers for: clear definitions, grace periods, and interim compliance requirements.
contract_threat_patterns.mitigation: Define ESG triggers precisely (lost vs pending vs expired). Include interim compliance obligations during grace period. Maximum cure period. Automatic suspension of preferred status during cure.
contract_threat_patterns.agent_use: Contract Reviewer: flag when ESG termination triggers have ambiguous definitions or unlimited cure periods.

contract_threat_patterns.name: CSDDD Due Diligence Cascade Failure
contract_threat_patterns.description: Corporate Sustainability Due Diligence Directive obligations not adequately cascaded through supply chain contracts.
contract_threat_patterns.attack_scenario: Large company subject to CSDDD has due diligence obligations for entire value chain. Key supplier contracts predate CSDDD and lack human rights and environmental due diligence provisions. Company cannot fulfil CSDDD obligations without contract renegotiation.
contract_threat_patterns.detection: Review supply contracts for CSDDD compliance. Check for human rights and environmental due diligence cascade. Verify remediation and termination provisions.
contract_threat_patterns.mitigation: Include CSDDD-compliant due diligence cascade in all supply contracts. Right to conduct human rights impact assessments. Require supplier prevention and remediation plans. Graduated response mechanism.
contract_threat_patterns.agent_use: STRIDE Enumerator: flag when supply contracts lack CSDDD-compliant due diligence cascade provisions.

contract_threat_patterns.name: Carbon Offset Credit Fraud in Contractual Commitments
contract_threat_patterns.description: Contractual net-zero commitments rely on carbon offset credits that are worthless, double-counted, or from fraudulent projects.
contract_threat_patterns.attack_scenario: Supplier commits to net-zero supply by 2030 using offset credits. Credits purchased from registry that later proves fraudulent (phantom forest projects). Customer net-zero claims invalidated. Regulatory and reputational damage.
contract_threat_patterns.detection: Review offset quality requirements. Check for recognised registry requirements (Gold Standard, Verra). Verify additionality and permanence criteria.
contract_threat_patterns.mitigation: Require offsets from recognised registries only. Include additionality and permanence requirements. Prioritise reduction over offsetting. Independent verification of offset quality. Replacement obligation for invalidated credits.
contract_threat_patterns.agent_use: Risk Assessor: flag when carbon commitments rely on offsets without quality standards or verification.

contract_threat_patterns.name: Living Wage Commitment Enforcement Gap
contract_threat_patterns.description: Contractual living wage commitments not effectively monitored or enforced in supply chain.
contract_threat_patterns.attack_scenario: Brand commits to living wage across supply chain. Tier-1 supplier signs living wage clause but pays minimum wage to agency workers who constitute 60 percent of workforce. Agency arrangement technically outside direct employment commitment.
contract_threat_patterns.detection: Review living wage provision scope. Check coverage of agency, temporary, and sub-contracted workers. Verify monitoring mechanism.
contract_threat_patterns.mitigation: Define living wage to cover all workers including agency and temporary. Require worker payroll verification. Unannounced audit rights. Include worker grievance hotline. Price adjustment mechanism to support living wage.
contract_threat_patterns.agent_use: Contract Reviewer: flag when living wage commitment excludes agency or temporary workers.

contract_threat_patterns.name: Biodiversity Net Gain Obligation Dispute
contract_threat_patterns.description: Developer disputes biodiversity net gain obligations or measurement methodology, delaying project completion.
contract_threat_patterns.attack_scenario: Development contract requires 10 percent biodiversity net gain. Developer argues baseline assessment overvalued existing habitat. Disputes measurement methodology. Refuses to implement additional mitigation. Planning condition remains unsatisfied.
contract_threat_patterns.detection: Review BNG obligations for: baseline assessment methodology, measurement standard, and dispute resolution for ecological assessments.
contract_threat_patterns.mitigation: Specify DEFRA biodiversity metric as measurement standard. Independent ecologist for baseline and completion assessment. Pre-agreed habitat banking arrangements. Escrow for long-term management costs.
contract_threat_patterns.agent_use: Risk Assessor: flag when biodiversity obligations lack specified measurement methodology or independent assessment.

contract_threat_patterns.name: Conflict Minerals Traceability Chain Break
contract_threat_patterns.description: Supply chain conflict minerals due diligence breaks at smelter level, creating EU Conflict Minerals Regulation non-compliance.
contract_threat_patterns.attack_scenario: Electronics manufacturer sources components with conflict mineral representations from tier-1 supplier. Smelter-level traceability not maintained. EU customs audit reveals non-compliant supply chain. Products detained at border.
contract_threat_patterns.detection: Review conflict minerals provisions for: smelter-level traceability, OECD due diligence guidance alignment, and RCOI obligations.
contract_threat_patterns.mitigation: Require RMAP-conformant smelter sourcing. Full chain of custody documentation. Annual conflict minerals report from all suppliers. Right to audit smelter information. Non-compliance triggers termination.
contract_threat_patterns.agent_use: STRIDE Enumerator: flag when conflict minerals obligations lack smelter-level traceability or OECD alignment.

contract_threat_patterns.name: Sanctions Evasion Through Trade Intermediary
contract_threat_patterns.description: Counter-party uses intermediary entities in non-sanctioned jurisdictions to circumvent trade sanctions.
contract_threat_patterns.attack_scenario: Customer sells technology to Dubai trading company. Trading company re-exports to sanctioned country through complex intermediary chain. Customer faces secondary sanctions exposure. Trading company had known links to sanctioned entities.
contract_threat_patterns.detection: Review end-use and end-user verification provisions. Check for re-export restrictions. Verify sanctions screening obligations cover beneficial ownership.
contract_threat_patterns.mitigation: End-use certification with re-export restrictions. Beneficial ownership screening. Right to terminate on reasonable suspicion of evasion. Annual sanctions compliance certification. Audit right over distribution chain.
contract_threat_patterns.agent_use: STRIDE Enumerator: flag when international trade contract lacks end-use verification or re-export restrictions.

contract_threat_patterns.name: Incoterm Risk Transfer Point Misunderstanding
contract_threat_patterns.description: Parties select inappropriate Incoterm creating unintended risk allocation for transit damage, insurance, and customs obligations.
contract_threat_patterns.attack_scenario: Buyer agrees FOB terms assuming seller responsible until goods reach buyer warehouse. FOB risk transfers at ship rail in port of loading. Goods damaged in transit. Buyer has no insurance for transit leg. Loss uninsured.
contract_threat_patterns.detection: Review Incoterm selection against actual logistics arrangement. Check insurance coverage matches risk transfer point. Verify customs obligation awareness.
contract_threat_patterns.mitigation: Match Incoterm to actual risk appetite and logistics capability. Ensure marine insurance covers full transit. Document customs obligations explicitly. Include Incoterm education requirement for procurement teams.
contract_threat_patterns.agent_use: Contract Reviewer: flag when Incoterm selection does not match party logistics capability or insurance coverage.

contract_threat_patterns.name: Letter of Credit Documentary Discrepancy Exploitation
contract_threat_patterns.description: Buyer exploits minor documentary discrepancies to refuse payment under letter of credit.
contract_threat_patterns.attack_scenario: Seller ships goods and presents LC documents. Buyer instructs bank to reject for minor discrepancy (company name abbreviated differently). By time discrepancy corrected, LC expired. Buyer has goods but no payment obligation under LC.
contract_threat_patterns.detection: Review LC terms for: discrepancy handling, amendment procedures, and presentation deadlines. Check document preparation standards.
contract_threat_patterns.mitigation: Align LC terms exactly with commercial contract. Pre-check documents before presentation. Include LC amendment mechanism. Require confirmed LC for high-risk jurisdictions. Back-up payment obligation outside LC.
contract_threat_patterns.agent_use: Risk Assessor: flag when international sale relies solely on LC payment without back-up payment obligation.

contract_threat_patterns.name: Dual-Use Goods Export Classification Dispute
contract_threat_patterns.description: Goods or technology reclassified as dual-use after contract formation, creating unexpected export control compliance burden.
contract_threat_patterns.attack_scenario: Company contracts to export AI processors to non-EU customer. Mid-contract, processor technology reclassified as dual-use under EU Regulation 2021/821. Export now requires licence. Customer in end-use risk country. Licence unlikely to be granted. Contract cannot be performed.
contract_threat_patterns.detection: Review export control classification and change provisions. Check for regulatory change risk allocation. Verify licence obligation responsibilities.
contract_threat_patterns.mitigation: Include regulatory change clause covering export control reclassification. Allocate licence application responsibility. Force majeure coverage for export denial. Termination right without penalty if export licence refused.
contract_threat_patterns.agent_use: Contract Reviewer: flag when contract for technology export lacks export control reclassification provisions.

contract_threat_patterns.name: Currency Hedging Obligation Mismatch
contract_threat_patterns.description: Long-term international contract with fixed pricing in one currency and costs in another, without adequate hedging provisions.
contract_threat_patterns.attack_scenario: UK company contracts to deliver services priced in EUR over 5 years. GBP weakens 20 percent against EUR. Cost base in GBP rises proportionally. No currency adjustment mechanism. Project becomes loss-making in year 3.
contract_threat_patterns.detection: Review currency provisions for long-term contracts. Check for adjustment mechanisms. Verify hedging obligation or cost pass-through.
contract_threat_patterns.mitigation: Include currency band (e.g., plus or minus 5 percent) with price adjustment outside band. Annual currency review mechanism. Option for hedging cost pass-through. Termination right for extreme currency movements.
contract_threat_patterns.agent_use: Risk Assessor: flag when multi-year international contract lacks currency adjustment mechanism.

contract_threat_patterns.name: Foreign Judgment Non-Enforcement Risk
contract_threat_patterns.description: Court judgment obtained in one jurisdiction unenforceable in jurisdiction where counter-party assets are located.
contract_threat_patterns.attack_scenario: UK company obtains GBP 5M judgment against Chinese supplier. China does not recognise UK court judgments. Supplier has no UK assets. Judgment is unenforceable. Legal costs wasted.
contract_threat_patterns.detection: Assess bilateral treaty coverage for judgment enforcement. Check if Hague Convention applies. Evaluate counter-party asset locations.
contract_threat_patterns.mitigation: Use ICC arbitration (New York Convention enforcement in 170+ countries). Include multi-jurisdiction enforcement clause. Require performance bond or bank guarantee from non-treaty jurisdictions.
contract_threat_patterns.agent_use: Risk Assessor: flag when governing law jurisdiction lacks treaty coverage for enforcement in counter-party country.

contract_threat_patterns.name: Local Content Requirement Non-Compliance
contract_threat_patterns.description: Failure to meet local content requirements in international contract leads to contract termination or penalty.
contract_threat_patterns.attack_scenario: Infrastructure contractor commits to 40 percent local content in African mining project. Imports skilled labour and materials from home country due to local capacity gaps. Host government audit reveals only 15 percent local content. Contract terminated. Mobilisation costs lost.
contract_threat_patterns.detection: Review local content obligations and measurement methodology. Check for force majeure or capacity gap provisions. Verify reporting requirements.
contract_threat_patterns.mitigation: Realistic local content targets with ramp-up schedule. Include capacity development obligation. Force majeure for genuine local capacity gaps. Regular reporting and verification. Graduated penalties rather than termination.
contract_threat_patterns.agent_use: Contract Reviewer: flag when local content targets lack ramp-up schedule or capacity gap provisions.

contract_threat_patterns.name: Sovereign Immunity Defence by State Entity
contract_threat_patterns.description: State-owned counter-party invokes sovereign immunity to avoid contractual obligations or arbitral award enforcement.
contract_threat_patterns.attack_scenario: Company wins ICC arbitration award against state-owned enterprise. SOE invokes sovereign immunity to resist enforcement. Assets claimed to be state property immune from execution. Award remains unenforced for years.
contract_threat_patterns.detection: Review waiver of immunity provisions. Check for commercial activity exception. Verify enforcement jurisdiction sovereign immunity rules.
contract_threat_patterns.mitigation: Include express waiver of sovereign immunity for both jurisdiction and enforcement. Specify commercial activity nature. Identify commercial (not sovereign) assets for enforcement. Choose enforcement-friendly jurisdictions.
contract_threat_patterns.agent_use: Risk Assessor: flag when contract with state entity lacks express sovereign immunity waiver.

contract_threat_patterns.name: Forced Technology Transfer Through Local Requirements
contract_threat_patterns.description: Host country regulations require technology transfer or local data storage that effectively forces disclosure of proprietary technology.
contract_threat_patterns.attack_scenario: Software company enters joint venture in host country requiring source code escrow with local regulator. Regulator shares escrow contents with domestic competitor. Company intellectual property effectively transferred to competitor.
contract_threat_patterns.detection: Review local law requirements for technology transfer. Check for source code disclosure obligations. Verify escrow agent independence.
contract_threat_patterns.mitigation: Limit technology transfer to operational necessity. Use compilation-only escrow rather than source code. Contractual restrictions on escrow use. Joint venture IP quarantine. Exit provisions allowing IP withdrawal.
contract_threat_patterns.agent_use: STRIDE Enumerator: flag when international joint venture requires source code or technology disclosure to government entities.

contract_threat_patterns.name: Anti-Bribery Supply Chain Exposure Through Agents
contract_threat_patterns.description: Local agent or distributor makes corrupt payments on company behalf, creating FCPA/UK Bribery Act liability.
contract_threat_patterns.attack_scenario: Company engages local agent for government contract in high-risk jurisdiction. Agent makes facilitation payments to officials. Company wins contract. Subsequent investigation reveals payments. Company faces prosecution under UK Bribery Act section 7.
contract_threat_patterns.detection: Review agent appointment provisions. Check for due diligence requirements, commission transparency, and compliance monitoring.
contract_threat_patterns.mitigation: Comprehensive agent due diligence. Commission paid only to verified bank accounts. Anti-bribery warranties and audit rights. Compliance training requirements. Termination right for any compliance concern. Indemnity for agent misconduct.
contract_threat_patterns.agent_use: STRIDE Enumerator: flag when agent appointment in high-risk jurisdiction lacks anti-bribery due diligence or audit rights.

contract_threat_patterns.name: Bid Rigging Through Consortium Arrangement
contract_threat_patterns.description: Consortium members collude to rotate winning bids across government contracts, maintaining artificial pricing.
contract_threat_patterns.attack_scenario: Three companies form rotating consortia for government IT contracts. Each takes turns as lead bidder at inflated prices. Losing bids are deliberately non-competitive. Government pays 30 percent above market rate for 5 years.
contract_threat_patterns.detection: Review consortium arrangements for: independence of bids, pricing transparency, and rotation patterns.
contract_threat_patterns.mitigation: Include anti-collusion declaration. Require independent bid preparation certification. Benchmark pricing against market rates. Audit trail for pricing decisions. Whistleblower mechanism for procurement fraud.
contract_threat_patterns.agent_use: Risk Assessor: flag when government contract involves consortium with history of rotating roles.

contract_threat_patterns.name: Revolving Door Conflict of Interest
contract_threat_patterns.description: Former government official joins contractor and uses insider knowledge to gain unfair advantage in procurement.
contract_threat_patterns.attack_scenario: Senior procurement official joins IT contractor 3 months after leaving government. Uses knowledge of upcoming requirements and evaluation criteria to tailor bids. Contractor wins series of contracts. Other bidders complain.
contract_threat_patterns.detection: Review conflict of interest provisions. Check for cooling-off period requirements. Verify ethical wall provisions for former officials.
contract_threat_patterns.mitigation: Require declaration of former government employment. 12-month cooling-off period for direct procurement involvement. Ethical wall preventing access to insider information. Termination right for undisclosed conflicts.
contract_threat_patterns.agent_use: Contract Reviewer: flag when government contract lacks cooling-off period or conflict of interest declarations.

contract_threat_patterns.name: Social Value Commitment Non-Delivery
contract_threat_patterns.description: Contractor makes ambitious social value commitments to win bid then fails to deliver, with no effective enforcement.
contract_threat_patterns.attack_scenario: Contractor commits to 30 percent local employment, 10 apprenticeships, and community investment to win social value-weighted procurement. Delivers 5 percent local employment, 2 apprenticeships, and no community investment. No contractual remedy available.
contract_threat_patterns.detection: Review social value obligations for: measurable KPIs, reporting requirements, and contractual remedies for non-delivery.
contract_threat_patterns.mitigation: Define social value as measurable KPIs tied to payment mechanism. Quarterly reporting against commitments. Service credits for underperformance. Social value delivery as condition for contract extension.
contract_threat_patterns.agent_use: Risk Assessor: flag when social value commitments lack measurable KPIs or contractual enforcement mechanism.

contract_threat_patterns.name: FOI Request Overriding Commercial Confidentiality
contract_threat_patterns.description: Freedom of Information request results in disclosure of commercially sensitive pricing and methodology despite confidentiality provisions.
contract_threat_patterns.attack_scenario: Competitor files FOI request for government contract pricing schedule. Government must disclose under transparency obligations. Contractor pricing methodology, margins, and resource costs disclosed. Competitor undercuts on all future bids.
contract_threat_patterns.detection: Review FOI provisions for: commercial confidentiality protections, consultation rights, and redaction procedures.
contract_threat_patterns.mitigation: Include pre-disclosure consultation right. Mark genuinely confidential information. Agree FOI redaction protocol. Structure pricing to protect methodology while allowing headline disclosure.
contract_threat_patterns.agent_use: Contract Reviewer: flag when government contract lacks FOI consultation right or commercial confidentiality protocol.

contract_threat_patterns.name: Gainshare-Painshare Mechanism Manipulation
contract_threat_patterns.description: Contractor manipulates baseline and measurement of gainshare-painshare mechanism to maximise gain and minimise pain.
contract_threat_patterns.attack_scenario: Contractor deliberately inflates initial baseline costs. Achieves normal efficiency. Reports as significant savings. Claims 50 percent gainshare on phantom savings. When costs overrun, argues baseline was understated. Government bears all pain.
contract_threat_patterns.detection: Review gainshare baseline setting methodology. Check for independent verification. Verify symmetry of sharing mechanism.
contract_threat_patterns.mitigation: Independent baseline verification. Annual benchmarking against market rates. Open book accounting. Symmetric sharing of both gains and losses. Cap on gainshare payments. Clawback for baseline manipulation.
contract_threat_patterns.agent_use: Risk Assessor: flag when gainshare mechanism lacks independent baseline verification or asymmetric sharing.

contract_threat_patterns.name: Security Clearance Withdrawal Disrupting Service
contract_threat_patterns.description: Key personnel security clearance revoked or not renewed, disrupting delivery of government contract.
contract_threat_patterns.attack_scenario: Lead consultant on classified government project has security clearance revoked after adverse personal circumstances. No cleared replacement available. Project halted for 6 months during replacement clearance process. Government invokes performance default.
contract_threat_patterns.detection: Review clearance provisions for: multiple cleared personnel requirement, clearance maintenance obligations, and replacement procedures.
contract_threat_patterns.mitigation: Maintain 150 percent cleared personnel ratio. Immediate notification of clearance issues. Pre-cleared replacement personnel identified. Clearance maintenance as contractual obligation. Grace period for replacement.
contract_threat_patterns.agent_use: Contract Reviewer: flag when classified government contract relies on single cleared individual without backup.

contract_threat_patterns.name: State Aid Recovery Risk for Contractor
contract_threat_patterns.description: Government contract pricing constitutes illegal state aid that may be subject to recovery, creating financial exposure for contractor.
contract_threat_patterns.attack_scenario: Government awards sole-source contract at above-market rates to support local employment. European Commission investigates as potential illegal state aid. Orders recovery of difference between contract price and market price. Contractor must repay EUR 5M.
contract_threat_patterns.detection: Review contract pricing against market rates. Check for state aid compliance provisions. Verify procurement process compliance.
contract_threat_patterns.mitigation: Include state aid compliance warranty from government. Market rate benchmarking evidence. Recovery indemnity from contracting authority. Termination right if state aid recovery ordered.
contract_threat_patterns.agent_use: Risk Assessor: flag when government contract pricing significantly exceeds market rates without state aid analysis.

contract_threat_patterns.name: Government Step-In Rights Overreach
contract_threat_patterns.description: Government exercises step-in rights in circumstances beyond intended scope, taking control of contractor operations.
contract_threat_patterns.attack_scenario: Minor service performance issue triggers emergency step-in. Government appoints replacement team at contractor expense. Government retains control for 6 months. Contractor continues paying but has no operational control. Quality actually declines under government management.
contract_threat_patterns.detection: Review step-in triggers for proportionality. Check for contractor rights during step-in. Verify step-out mechanism and cost allocation.
contract_threat_patterns.mitigation: Define step-in triggers narrowly and proportionately. Include cure period before step-in. Time-limit step-in period. Contractor right to propose remediation plan. Cost allocation based on fault. Clear step-out criteria.
contract_threat_patterns.agent_use: Contract Reviewer: flag when government step-in rights lack proportionality triggers or time limits.

contract_threat_patterns.name: Unfunded Continuous Improvement Obligations
contract_threat_patterns.description: Government contract requires continuous improvement delivering year-on-year savings without additional funding for innovation.
contract_threat_patterns.attack_scenario: Contract requires 3 percent annual efficiency savings over 10 years. By year 5, contractor has exhausted easy efficiencies. Must invest GBP 2M in automation to achieve further savings. Contract provides no innovation funding. Contractor cuts quality to meet savings target.
contract_threat_patterns.detection: Review continuous improvement obligations for: funding mechanism, innovation investment sharing, and quality protection.
contract_threat_patterns.mitigation: Include innovation fund contribution from both parties. Set realistic improvement targets diminishing over time. Quality floor below which savings cannot reduce service. Investment cost sharing mechanism.
contract_threat_patterns.agent_use: Risk Assessor: flag when continuous improvement obligations lack innovation funding or quality floor.

contract_threat_patterns.name: Government TUPE Transfer Cost Underestimation
contract_threat_patterns.description: Government provides incomplete TUPE information leading contractor to underestimate employment costs and win bid on incorrect basis.
contract_threat_patterns.attack_scenario: Government outsources service. Provides ELI showing 50 employees. Actual transfer includes 20 additional agency workers with continuity of employment rights, unfunded pension liabilities of GBP 3M, and 5 pending tribunal claims.
contract_threat_patterns.detection: Review TUPE information completeness. Check for: agency workers, pension liabilities, pending claims, and custom terms. Verify warranty on ELI accuracy.
contract_threat_patterns.mitigation: Require comprehensive ELI including agency workers 28+ days before bid submission. Government indemnity for pre-transfer liabilities and inaccurate ELI. Pension protection provisions. Price adjustment mechanism for undisclosed costs.
contract_threat_patterns.agent_use: Risk Assessor: flag when government outsourcing contract lacks comprehensive TUPE information warranty or pre-transfer indemnity.
